text.title: Diary Study Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Diary Study Basics methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Every non-trivial field in the produced artefact MUST carry >=1 verbatim citation from an auditable source (URL, transcript_id, ticket id, dashboard snapshot). Fields with no anchor are rejected as fabrication.

rationale.source: Anti-hallucination practice for Diary Study Basics

rule.rationale: LLMs cheerfully invent plausible values without inputs; verbatim anchoring makes the artefact falsifiable and a downstream reviewer can re-pull the source.

rule.statement: Every artefact MUST carry a single named owner and an ISO-8601 `last_touched` timestamp. Generic owners ("team", "us", "tbd", "n/a") and empty timestamps are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Un-owned artefacts metastasize and the operator stops trusting the whole library; a named owner is the minimum precondition for follow-through.

rule.statement: Each filled instance MUST record `template_version` matching the methodology frontmatter at write time. Downstream consumers parse legacy artefacts against this version, never against "latest".
rule.rationale: Templates without version pinning become a one-way ratchet; old instances become unreadable when the template drifts.

rule.statement: Before any binding action driven by this artefact (commit, send, ramp, freeze, escalation, ledger write, contract sign), a named human reviewer MUST approve. The agent surfaces a `ready_for_review` flag and never sets `status=approved` itself.
rule.rationale: Self-approval defeats the human checkpoint and the audit trail. The status enum exists precisely to make the human signature observable.

rule.statement: If the prerequisites in the AGENTS.md table cannot all be satisfied, the agent MUST skip the methodology and emit a structured `skip` decision pointing to the missing prerequisite, not produce a degraded artefact.
rule.rationale: Degraded artefacts pollute the library because they look complete; an explicit skip preserves the audit signal that the question was asked.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running the methodology under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
